---
title: "Welcome"
webr:  
  show-startup-message: false   # Display status of webR initialization
---

Welcome to the casual introduction to Causal Inference!


In this lesson, students will learn what the study of causal inference is as well as why randomized experiments are considered the golden standard.

Students will be able to:

1.  Define what is the study of causal inference.

2.  Identify the difference between randomized experiments and observational studies.

3.  Execute a difference–in-means calculation to estimate treatment effect.




```{webr-r}
print("Hello")
```

## In Python

```{pyodide-python}

import numpy as np

X = np.array([[1], [2], [3]])
y = np.array([2, 4, 6])
print(y)
```
